behaviors.description: STT output preserves speaker labels for downstream attendee attribution.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Failure is not just parser logic; it appears when real diarization output is wrong/missing and handoff breaks attribution. REAL-INFRA: external API exists, but core risk is model output content, not transport. MOCK-HIDE: stubbed STT labels would mask diarization failures. LLM-DEP: yes (speech model output quality). MULTI-STEP: no required multi-agent sequence to validate this behavior itself. Rejected Workflow because behavior text targets STT label preservation, not a required chain of multiple model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run real multi-speaker meeting audio through the production STT API with speaker-labeling enabled, feed result into attribution path, and assert each quoted/assigned statement retains a non-empty, correct speaker label mapping used by summarization input.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into summary-input structure.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is parser/extraction logic on transcript text; no required cross-module invariant beyond function contract. REAL-INFRA: none. MOCK-HIDE: mocking not required. LLM-DEP: none for this behavior. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on deterministic extraction into a structure, which can fail within one local parser boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Given transcript fixtures containing explicit assignments (including edge punctuation/formatting), call the parser function and assert extracted items include correct assignee and task text fields in the expected input schema.

behaviors.description: Live summary includes at least one action item per attendee who was assigned a task.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requirement is about final summary content coverage per attendee, not just routing code. REAL-INFRA: network call may exist, but key failure is model decision quality. MOCK-HIDE: fixed mock summary can trivially satisfy coverage and hide real misses. LLM-DEP: yes, depends on real model generating/retaining action items per assignee. MULTI-STEP: not inherently required if transcript is provided directly to summarizer. Rejected Workflow because behavior text can be validated on one live summarization step.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcript(s) with known assigned tasks across multiple attendees, call live summarization, and assert the output action_items contains at least one item attributed to each attendee who received an assignment in ground truth.

behaviors.description: Identified decisions are routed to `decisions` schema field, not generic narrative.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (decision-identification output -> schema assembler), a cross-module invariant. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. MOCK-HIDE: pure unit of one function may miss contract mismatches between wired modules. LLM-DEP: behavior text says 'identified decisions' (already identified), so model quality is out of scope. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Agentic because concrete text disqualifier is that identification is precomputed; this test targets routing/orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real decision-extraction output object (or deterministic fixture in extractor output format) through the schema-building pipeline and assert decision entries land in `decisions` array while `narrative` excludes those entries.

behaviors.description: Key decisions from transcript appear in live summary `decisions` section, not only narrative.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: requirement depends on model selecting what is 'key' and placing it correctly in generated structure. REAL-INFRA: external call exists, but central risk is content quality. MOCK-HIDE: mocked model output would always place decisions correctly and hide misses. LLM-DEP: yes. MULTI-STEP: not necessarily; can be validated in one summarization call from transcript text. Rejected Workflow because behavior text does not require sequential multi-agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Provide real transcripts with known key decisions, run live summarization model, and assert each expected key decision is represented in `decisions` section (semantic match), with failures if only present in narrative/free-text.

behaviors.description: For audio >60 minutes, service segments audio, processes sequentially, and merges into one coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug can emerge across stage boundaries (segmenter -> per-segment STT/summarize -> merger) and sequence state. REAL-INFRA: long-audio processing may involve real streaming/files, but higher boundary is multi-agentic orchestration. MOCK-HIDE: mocked segment summaries can hide coherence/continuity failures after real model passes. LLM-DEP: yes for summary coherence and cross-segment consolidation. MULTI-STEP: yes, repeated agentic steps in sequence by definition. Rejected Agentic because behavior explicitly requires chained segment processing plus merge, i.e., pipeline-level sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute meeting recording through production segmentation and sequential processing, then validate merged output for schema correctness plus cross-segment coherence checks (no dropped major topics/actions, no duplicate/conflicting decisions from segment boundaries).

behaviors.description: End-to-end pipeline (upload, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) runs automatically for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is explicit full-pipeline behavior with multiple stages and handoffs. REAL-INFRA: includes real upload/I/O and external APIs. MOCK-HIDE: stubs would mask operational and model interaction failures. LLM-DEP: yes, real summarization/STT calls in pipeline path. MULTI-STEP: yes, multiple agentic steps in sequence with delivery completion criterion. Rejected System because system-level single-boundary tests cannot verify multi-agentic orchestration correctness across all stages.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like E2E run on a real meeting audio file: upload, real STT call, real LLM summarization, and output delivery; assert successful completion, valid structured schema, and no manual retries/interventions.
